+    func testDebugLogStorePrunesPersistedEntriesOnInitialization() throws {
+        let logURL = makeTemporaryFileURL()
+        let entries = (0..<251).map { index in
+            DebugLogEntry(
+                id: UUID(uuidString: String(format: "00000000-0000-0000-0000-%012d", index))!,
+                timestamp: Date(timeIntervalSince1970: TimeInterval(index)),
+                category: .performance,
+                message: "Entry \(index)"
+            )
+        }
+        let payload = try JSONEncoder().encode(entries)
+        try payload.write(to: logURL)
+
+        let store = DebugLogStore(url: logURL)
+
+        let persistedEntries = store.loadEntries()
+        XCTAssertEqual(persistedEntries.count, 250)
+        XCTAssertEqual(persistedEntries.first?.message, "Entry 1")
+        XCTAssertEqual(persistedEntries.last?.message, "Entry 250")
+    }
+

@@ -0,0 +1,106 @@
+import XCTest
+@testable import GhostPepper
+
+final class ArchivedAudioCodecTests: XCTestCase {
+    func testRawFloat32SerializationRoundTripsLosslessly() throws {
+        let samples: [Float] = [0.25, -0.5, 0.0, 1.0]
+
+        let data = ArchivedAudioCodec.serializeRawFloat32(samples)
+        let decoded = try ArchivedAudioCodec.deserialize(data)
+
+        XCTAssertEqual(decoded, samples)
+    }
+
+    func testRawFloat32DeserializationRejectsMisalignedData() {
+        XCTAssertThrowsError(try ArchivedAudioCodec.deserialize(Data([0x00, 0x01, 0x02]))) { error in
+            XCTAssertEqual(error as? SerializedAudioError, .invalidSerializedAudioData)
+        }
+    }
+
+    func testWAVSerializationRoundTripsWithinTolerance() throws {
+        let samples: [Float] = [-1.0, -0.25, 0.0, 0.25, 1.0]
+
+        let data = ArchivedAudioCodec.serializeWAV(samples)
+        let decoded = try ArchivedAudioCodec.deserialize(data)
+
+        XCTAssertEqual(String(decoding: data.prefix(4), as: UTF8.self), "RIFF")
+        XCTAssertEqual(String(decoding: data.subdata(in: 8..<12), as: UTF8.self), "WAVE")
+        XCTAssertEqual(decoded.count, samples.count)
+        for (lhs, rhs) in zip(decoded, samples) {
+            XCTAssertEqual(lhs, rhs, accuracy: 0.0001)
+        }
+    }
+
+    func testWAVDeserializationSupportsOddSizedChunksAndArbitraryChunkOrdering() throws {
+        let data = makeWAVDataWithJunkAndDataBeforeFormat()
+
+        let decoded = try ArchivedAudioCodec.deserialize(data)
+
+        XCTAssertEqual(decoded.count, 1)
+        XCTAssertEqual(decoded[0], 1.0, accuracy: 0.0001)
+    }
+
+    func testWAVDeserializationRejectsInvalidHeaders() {
+        let invalidData = Data("not-a-wave-file".utf8)
+
+        XCTAssertThrowsError(try ArchivedAudioCodec.deserialize(invalidData)) { error in
+            XCTAssertEqual(error as? SerializedAudioError, .invalidSerializedAudioData)
+        }
+    }
+
+    private func makeWAVDataWithJunkAndDataBeforeFormat() -> Data {
+        var chunks = Data()
+
+        chunks.appendASCII("data")
+        chunks.appendUInt32LE(2)
+        chunks.appendInt16LE(Int16.max)
+
+        chunks.appendASCII("JUNK")
+        chunks.appendUInt32LE(3)
+        chunks.append(contentsOf: [0x01, 0x02, 0x03])
+        chunks.append(0x00)
+
+        chunks.appendASCII("fmt ")
+        chunks.appendUInt32LE(16)
+        chunks.appendUInt16LE(1)
+        chunks.appendUInt16LE(1)
+        chunks.appendUInt32LE(16_000)
+        chunks.appendUInt32LE(32_000)
+        chunks.appendUInt16LE(2)
+        chunks.appendUInt16LE(16)
+
+        var data = Data()
+        data.appendASCII("RIFF")
+        data.appendUInt32LE(UInt32(4 + chunks.count))
+        data.appendASCII("WAVE")
+        data.append(chunks)
+        return data
+    }
+}
+
+private extension Data {
+    mutating func appendASCII(_ string: String) {
+        append(string.data(using: .ascii)!)
+    }
+
+    mutating func appendUInt16LE(_ value: UInt16) {
+        var littleEndian = value.littleEndian
+        Swift.withUnsafeBytes(of: &littleEndian) { bytes in
+            append(contentsOf: bytes)
+        }
+    }
+
+    mutating func appendUInt32LE(_ value: UInt32) {
+        var littleEndian = value.littleEndian
+        Swift.withUnsafeBytes(of: &littleEndian) { bytes in
+            append(contentsOf: bytes)
+        }
+    }
+
+    mutating func appendInt16LE(_ value: Int16) {
+        var littleEndian = value.littleEndian
+        Swift.withUnsafeBytes(of: &littleEndian) { bytes in
+            append(contentsOf: bytes)
+        }
+    }
+}

@@ -50,6 +50,28 @@ final class DictationSessionTraceTests: XCTestCase {
 
         XCTAssertEqual(logger.messages, [])
     }
+
+    func testTraceExposesRecordedStageDurations() {
+        let builder = LiveDictationSessionTraceBuilder(
+            dateProvider: SequencedDateProvider(dates: [
+                Date(timeIntervalSince1970: 0.0),
+                Date(timeIntervalSince1970: 0.35),
+                Date(timeIntervalSince1970: 0.6),
+                Date(timeIntervalSince1970: 0.9),
+            ]),
+            logger: RecordingPerformanceTraceLogger()
+        )
+
+        let trace = builder.makeTrace()
+        trace.markTranscriptionStart()
+        trace.markTranscriptionEnd()
+        trace.markCleanupStart()
+        trace.markCleanupEnd()
+
+        let durations = trace.stageDurations()
+        XCTAssertEqual(try XCTUnwrap(durations.transcriptionDuration), 0.35, accuracy: 0.0001)
+        XCTAssertEqual(try XCTUnwrap(durations.cleanupDuration), 0.3, accuracy: 0.0001)
+    }
 }
 
 private final class SequencedDateProvider: DateProviding {

@@ -111,6 +120,37 @@ final class TranscriptionLabArchiveStoreTests: XCTestCase {
         XCTAssertFalse(FileManager.default.fileExists(atPath: rootURL.path()))
     }
 
+    func testArchiveOmitsOptionalFieldsWhenTheyAreNil() throws {
+        let rootURL = makeTemporaryRootURL()
+        let store = TranscriptionLabArchiveStore(rootURL: rootURL)
+        let request = RecordingArchiveRequest(
+            id: UUID(uuidString: "33333333-3333-3333-3333-333333333333")!,
+            createdAt: Date(timeIntervalSince1970: 300),
+            audioSamples: [0.1, 0.2],
+            speechModelID: "openai_whisper-small.en",
+            cleanupModelName: "Cleanup disabled",
+            cleanupUsedFallback: false
+        )
+
+        try store.archive(request)
+
+        let entry = try XCTUnwrap(store.loadEntries().first)
+        XCTAssertNil(entry.windowContents)
+        XCTAssertNil(entry.rawTranscription)
+        XCTAssertNil(entry.correctedTranscription)
+        XCTAssertNil(entry.diarizationSummary)
+
+        let indexURL = rootURL.appending(path: "transcription-lab-index.json", directoryHint: .notDirectory)
+        let indexPayload = try XCTUnwrap(
+            JSONSerialization.jsonObject(with: Data(contentsOf: indexURL)) as? [[String: Any]]
+        )
+        let encodedEntry = try XCTUnwrap(indexPayload.first)
+        XCTAssertNil(encodedEntry["windowContents"])
+        XCTAssertNil(encodedEntry["rawTranscription"])
+        XCTAssertNil(encodedEntry["correctedTranscription"])
+        XCTAssertNil(encodedEntry["diarizationSummary"])
+    }
+
     func testCorruptArchiveDeletesIndexTimingsAndAudioDirectory() throws {
         let rootURL = makeTemporaryRootURL()
         let audioDirectoryURL = rootURL.appending(path: "audio", directoryHint: .isDirectory)
@@ -171,6 +211,18 @@ final class TranscriptionLabArchiveStoreTests: XCTestCase {
         )
     }
 
+    func testAudioURLResolvesWithinArchiveAudioDirectory() {
+        let rootURL = makeTemporaryRootURL()
+        let store = TranscriptionLabArchiveStore(rootURL: rootURL)
+
+        XCTAssertEqual(
+            store.audioURL(forAudioFileName: "clip.wav"),
+            rootURL
+                .appending(path: "audio", directoryHint: .isDirectory)
+                .appending(path: "clip.wav", directoryHint: .notDirectory)
+        )
+    }
+
     private func makeTemporaryRootURL() -> URL {
         let rootURL = FileManager.default.temporaryDirectory
             .appending(path: UUID().uuidString, directoryHint: .isDirectory)

@@ -170,3 +170,13 @@
 - Wired speaker filtering through `AppController`, settings, and archive persistence so eligible FluidAudio recordings prepare diarization coordinators, prefer non-empty filtered transcripts, degrade cleanly to full-buffer transcription on diarizer failures, and persist diarization summaries plus fallback metadata into transcription-lab history.
 - Closed the iteration audit by tracing the already-implemented toggle-to-talk engine and restart behavior into this iteration's requirement set, then moved the transcription-lab detail-view visualization stories `STORY-0695` through `STORY-0699` into `ITER-0018` because those UI surfaces still belong to the later history/lab slice rather than to the runtime delivered here.
 **Summary:** Delivered the speaker-filtering and toggle-dictation slice on top of the hardened runtime. Ghost Pepper now lazily loads and reuses Sortformer diarization models for supported FluidAudio recordings, feeds every live chunk through a per-session diarizer, prefers target-speaker transcription before falling back to full-buffer speech, persists the resulting diarization summary for later history use, and traces the existing exact-chord toggle/start/stop behavior to this iteration's completed scope. The only scope moved forward during wrap-up was the later transcription-lab visualization work for diarization details, which remains pending in `ITER-0018`.
+
+## ITER-0017
+
+**Completed:** 2026-04-10
+**Stories delivered:** STORY-0800, STORY-0801, STORY-0802, STORY-0803, STORY-0804, STORY-0805, STORY-0806, STORY-0807, STORY-0808, STORY-0809, STORY-0810, STORY-0811, STORY-0812, STORY-0813, STORY-0814, STORY-0815, STORY-0816, STORY-0817, STORY-0818, STORY-0819, STORY-0820, STORY-0821, STORY-0822, STORY-0823, STORY-0824, STORY-0825, STORY-0826, STORY-0827, STORY-0828, STORY-0829, STORY-0830, STORY-0831, STORY-0832, STORY-0833, STORY-0834
+**Tasks executed:**
+- Added a shared archived-audio codec with raw Float32 and strict WAV serialization/deserialization coverage, then replaced the ad-hoc archive-only WAV encoder with the new codec path.
+- Hardened live archival metadata and persistence by storing structured diarization summaries, reconstructing archived window context from its plain-text JSON field, recording trace-derived transcription and cleanup timings, resolving archived audio URLs, and keeping failed-transcription sessions archived with nil text instead of synthetic empty strings.
+- Closed the remaining persistence audit gaps by pruning persisted debug logs to the documented cap during initialization, tracing unlimited model-cache and immutable history-entry behavior into this iteration, and moving `STORY-0799` to `ITER-0019` because its debug-viewer-gated snapshot depends on the later diagnostics slice instead of the archive store itself.
+**Summary:** Delivered the recording-history and archiving slice on top of the hardened runtime. Ghost Pepper now archives live sessions as playable 16 kHz mono WAV files with structured JSON metadata, reconstructs archived window context objects from the persisted plain-text field, stores separate transcription and cleanup timings alongside the entry index, prunes persisted debug logs and history entries to their documented bounds, and preserves failed-transcription sessions without inventing empty archived text. During wrap-up, `STORY-0503` was removed from this iteration because it was already completed in `ITER-0005`, and `STORY-0799` moved to `ITER-0019` because the viewer-gated debug snapshot remains part of later observability work.
